file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Update Plan page of the Instant Ink application. Contains comprehensive locator definitions for all UI elements related to plan management including plan details card, plan selection cards for different page tiers (10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700, 1500 pages), plan upgrade and downgrade modals, pause/resume plan functionality, and various action links. Provides helper methods to dynamically retrieve plan elements by page count or position.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the Update Plan page where users can view their current plan details, change to different page tier plans, upgrade or downgrade plans, pause or resume plans, manage shipping and billing information, redeem prepaid codes, and cancel their Instant Ink subscription. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and element management functionality. Contains 60+ locator definitions organized by functional areas and provides dynamic helper methods to access plan elements programmatically.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the UpdatePlanPage by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then systematically defines all 60+ locator selectors as attributes on the self.elements object. The locators are organized into logical groups: Plan Details card elements (plan_details_card, replace_printer_link, cancel_instant_ink, cancel_paper_add_on, contact_support_link, change_shipping_link, change_billing_info_link, prepaid_code_link, free_trial_plan_details, paper_free_months, pause_plan_info, resume_plan_link, special_offers_balance, plan_information), Plan Cards for each tier (plan_10 through plan_1500 using container position indices 0-7), Downgrade Plan Modal elements (downgrade_plan_confirmation, cancel_change_plan_button, cancel_change_plan_link, undo_plan_change_cancel_button, undo_plan_change_confirmation, change_plan_button, plan_downgrade_close_modal_button), Upgrade Plan Modal elements (upgrade_plan_confirmation, upgrade_next_month, upgrade_current_month), Pause Plan elements (plan_100_resume_tag, plan_100_resume_button), Header elements (header_cancel_link, header_remove_paper_link), and Page elements (page_title, plan_card_container, free_trial_info_card, disclaimer_card, print_history_link, terms_and_service_link, pending_change_plan), and individual change plan buttons for each tier (change_plan_button_10 through change_plan_button_1500, dashboard_plan_selector_paper_card_button). All locators use data-testid attributes for maximum reliability except for a few using class selectors with starts-with patterns.

parameters.name: pages
parameters.description: The page count tier for the plan (10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700, or 1500)

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically retrieves the change plan button selector for a specific page tier by constructing the attribute name as 'change_plan_button_{pages}' where pages is the provided integer. Uses hasattr() to check if the constructed attribute name exists on the self object. If the attribute exists, returns the selector value using getattr(self, plan_attr). If the attribute does not exist (meaning an invalid page count was provided), raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating that the plan with the specified page count was not found in UpdatePlanPage. This method enables programmatic access to plan buttons without hardcoding specific page tiers in test code.

parameters.name: plan_number
parameters.description: The zero-based position index of the plan card (0-7)

functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright Locator object for a plan card based on its position index in the plan selector container. Constructs a data-testid selector string dynamically using f-string formatting: f"[data-testid='plans-selector-plan-card-container-{plan_number}']" where plan_number is the provided integer position (0 for 10 pages plan, 1 for 25 pages, 2 for 50 pages, etc.). Calls self.page.locator() with the constructed selector string to create and return a Playwright Locator object that can be used for interactions. This method is useful when the exact page tier is unknown but the position in the UI is known, or when iterating through all plan cards programmatically.

parameters.name: pages
parameters.description: The page count tier for the plan (10, 25, 50, 100, 300, 500, 700, or 1500)

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically retrieves the plan card container selector for a specific page tier by constructing the attribute name as 'plan_{pages}' where pages is the provided integer. Uses hasattr() to check if the constructed attribute name exists on the self object. If the attribute exists, returns the selector value using getattr(self, plan_attr). If the attribute does not exist (meaning an invalid page count was provided), raises a ValueError with a descriptive message indicating that the plan with the specified page count was not found in UpdatePlanPage. This method enables programmatic access to plan card containers by page tier without hardcoding specific values in test code.

Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the cancel Instant Ink link. Accesses the cancel_instant_ink selector from self.elements.cancel_instant_ink and passes it to self.page.locator() to create a Locator. Uses the .last property on the Locator to select the last matching element on the page, which is useful when multiple cancel links exist and the last one is the relevant one (possibly due to DOM structure or multiple instances). Returns the Locator object that can be used for click, visibility checks, or other interactions. This property provides a convenient way to access the cancel link without calling a method.

Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the free trial information card. Accesses the free_trial_info_card selector from self.elements.free_trial_info_card and passes it to self.page.locator() to create a Locator. Uses the .first property on the Locator to select the first matching element on the page, which is useful when multiple trial info cards might exist and the first one is the primary one to interact with. Returns the Locator object that can be used for visibility checks, text extraction, or other interactions. This property provides a convenient way to access the trial info card without calling a method.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object follows a comprehensive locator organization pattern with 60+ UI element definitions covering all aspects of plan management. The class uses the self.elements pattern inherited from BasePageObject to store all locators as attributes, which is a clean separation of locator definitions from interaction logic. Notable design patterns include: (1) Systematic naming convention for plan tiers using position indices (plan_10, plan_25, etc.) and corresponding change buttons (change_plan_button_10, etc.), (2) Three dynamic helper methods (get_select_plan_button, get_plan_by_position, get_plan_by_pages) that enable programmatic access to plan elements without hardcoding, (3) Two property methods (cancel_instant_ink, free_trial_info_card) that use .last and .first selectors to handle multiple matching elements, (4) Extensive use of data-testid attributes for reliable element identification, (5) Some composite selectors combining data-testid with descendant selectors for precise targeting, (6) Use of CSS attribute starts-with selectors (^=) for flexible matching of dynamic elements. The page object supports complex workflows including plan upgrades, downgrades, pausing, resuming, cancellation, and various account management tasks. The helper methods make this page object highly reusable for data-driven tests that need to interact with different plan tiers dynamically.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Test data payload for Real-Time Printer Data Service (RTPDS) representing an Instant Ink Cartridge (IIC) printer configuration with four separate color cartridges (Yellow, Magenta, Cyan, Black). Contains comprehensive printer state information including device identification, firmware version, supply status for each cartridge, remote attestation data, impression counts, and service configuration. Used for simulating printer status reports and testing ink supply fulfillment workflows in the Instant Ink system.
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This JSON payload represents a complete RTPDS (Real-Time Printer Data Service) message for an IIC (Instant Ink Cartridge) printer with four separate color cartridges. The structure contains several key sections: (1) Device metadata including entityId (cloud_id template variable), firmware version, trigger reason 'lop' (likely 'low on pages'), and sequence numbers for configuration and credentials tracking. (2) Remote attestation section with cryptographic signature, session data, and attestation data map defining offsets and lengths for various counter fields - this is used for secure verification of printer state. (3) Remote attestation overrides section with template variables for totalImpressionCount, bizlogicImpressionCount, internalReportImpressionCount, and snapshotCountersSum - these allow test data injection. (4) Supply status section containing detailed state for four cartridges (Y=Yellow, M=Magenta, C=Cyan, K=Black), each with identical structure including: colorCode, inkSuggestedValidations=['ok'], suggestedValidations=['ok'], empty inkViolations array, extensive inkDetailInfo object with 20+ fields describing cartridge properties (isTrial, tradeMark, timeTravel flags, subBrand, supplyKeyDescription, safeStop, persoRevision, selectabilityNumber, ssa, class, supplyType, majorAccount, isUsed, templateMajor, modelNumber, hostTradeId, suggestedMessage, familyId), insertionState='ack', fulfillmentState='requestSupplyFulfillment', pctLevelRemaining=100, consumableUniqueId with template variable {{uuid}} plus color suffix, deviceAssessment='genuineHp', pct10LevelRemaining=100, supplyModelType='starter', isRefill='false', region=61750. All four cartridges have identical configuration except for colorCode and consumableUniqueId suffix. The payload uses multiple template variables ({{cloud_id}}, {{lastValidConfigSeqNum}}, {{lastReceivedConfigSeqNum}}, {{lastValidCredentialSeqNum}}, {{UniqueTimestamp}}, {{totalImpressionCount}}, {{bizlogicImpressionCount}}, {{internalReportImpressionCount}}, {{snapshotCountersSum}}, {{crsSessionData}}, {{uuid}}, {{supplyInsertionCount}}) that must be replaced with actual values during test execution. This payload is used to simulate a printer reporting its status to the Instant Ink service, triggering supply fulfillment workflows when cartridges are low or empty.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Test data payload for Real-Time Printer Data Service (RTPDS) representing an Instant Ink Printer Head (IPH) printer configuration with two combined cartridges (CMY tri-color and K black). Contains comprehensive printer state information including device identification, firmware version, supply status for each cartridge, remote attestation data, impression counts, and service configuration. Used for simulating printer status reports and testing ink supply fulfillment workflows for printers with integrated printhead cartridges in the Instant Ink system.
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This JSON payload represents a complete RTPDS (Real-Time Printer Data Service) message for an IPH (Instant Ink Printer Head) printer with two combined cartridges instead of four separate ones. The key difference from the IIC payload is in the supplyStatus.supplyStates section which contains only two cartridges: CMY (tri-color combining Cyan, Magenta, Yellow) and K (black). The structure is otherwise identical to the IIC payload with the same sections: (1) Device metadata with entityId, firmware version, trigger reason, and sequence numbers. (2) Remote attestation section with identical cryptographic signature, session data, and attestation data map. (3) Remote attestation overrides with the same template variables for impression counts. (4) Supply status section with two cartridges (CMY and K) instead of four, each having the same detailed structure as IIC cartridges including colorCode, inkSuggestedValidations, suggestedValidations, inkViolations, inkDetailInfo with 20+ fields, insertionState='ack', fulfillmentState='requestSupplyFulfillment', pctLevelRemaining=100, consumableUniqueId with template variables ({{uuid}}-supply-cmy and {{uuid}}-supply-k), deviceAssessment='genuineHp', pct10LevelRemaining=100, supplyModelType='starter', isRefill='false', region=61750. The CMY cartridge represents a tri-color printhead cartridge common in consumer inkjet printers where cyan, magenta, and yellow inks are combined in a single replaceable unit. This payload uses the same template variables as IIC and is used to simulate printer status reports for IPH printer models in the Instant Ink system. The distinction between IIC and IPH payloads is critical for testing different printer hardware configurations and their corresponding supply fulfillment logic.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Empty Python package initialization file that marks the test_flows_common directory as a Python package, allowing modules within this directory to be imported using standard Python import statements. This file contains no code but its presence is required by Python's package system.
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a standard empty __init__.py file with zero bytes. Its sole purpose is to designate the test_flows_common directory as a Python package. Without this file, Python would not recognize the directory as a package and modules within it could not be imported. The file contains no executable code, no imports, no classes, no functions, and no constants. This is a common pattern in Python projects to organize related modules into packages. The test_flows_common package appears to contain test data files (JSON payloads and address data) used across multiple test flows in the AURA framework.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive test data file containing shipping address information for 32 countries/regions supported by HP Instant Ink service. Each country has 3 address variations: 2 valid addresses with real postal codes and 1 invalid address with a fake postal code for negative testing. Used for testing address validation, shipping information updates, account creation, and international shipping workflows across all supported Instant Ink markets.
file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a large JSON file (49,810 bytes) containing a single root object with 32 country keys. Each country key maps to an array of 3 address objects. The countries included are: Australia, Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Canada, Croatia, Cyprus, Czechia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hong Kong, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Puerto Rico, Romania, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, United Kingdom, and United States. Each address object has a consistent structure with the following fields: firstName (always 'InstantInk'), lastName (always 'User'), company (usually empty string), street1 (primary address line), street2 (secondary address line, often empty), city, state (state/province code, sometimes empty for countries without states), fullState (full state/province name, sometimes empty), countryCode (2-letter ISO country code), zipCode (postal code), phoneNumber1 (phone number with country code), ignoreValidationErrors (always true), optedIn (always true), isDefaultAddress (always true for first 2 addresses, false for 3rd in US). The pattern for each country is: Address 1 and 2 have valid, real postal codes for actual locations (often HP office addresses), while Address 3 has an invalid or non-standard postal code (like '2345', '1234', '12345', '00120', 'ABC1234', 'GY00 0QZ', '88877') for testing validation error handling. Some countries have locale-specific variations like Hong Kong with fullState_en-HK and fullState_zh-HK for bilingual support. The addresses include special characters and non-ASCII characters for countries like Greece (Greek alphabet), Bulgaria (Cyrillic), and various European countries with diacritics, which is important for testing internationalization and character encoding. Phone numbers are formatted with country codes (e.g., '61282784492' for Australia, '4313400210100' for Austria, '8882060289' for Canada). The ignoreValidationErrors flag set to true suggests these addresses are used in test environments where strict validation might be bypassed. This data structure is ideal for parameterized tests that need to verify shipping address functionality across all supported markets, test address validation logic for both valid and invalid inputs, verify international character handling, and ensure the application correctly processes addresses from all 32 supported countries.

UpdatePlanPage.get_select_plan_button — Dynamically retrieve change plan button by page tier
UpdatePlanPage.get_plan_by_position — Get plan card locator by position index
UpdatePlanPage.get_plan_by_pages — Get plan card locator by page count tier
UpdatePlanPage.cancel_instant_ink (property) — Get cancel Instant Ink link locator
UpdatePlanPage.free_trial_info_card (property) — Get free trial info card locator
key_reusable_locators: UpdatePlanPage.cancel_instant_ink — Cancel Instant Ink subscription link
UpdatePlanPage.plan_10 through plan_1500 — Plan card containers for all tiers
UpdatePlanPage.change_plan_button_10 through change_plan_button_1500 — Change plan buttons for all tiers
UpdatePlanPage.upgrade_next_month — Apply upgrade next billing cycle option
key_reusable_test_data: RTPDS-PayLoad-IIC.json — RTPDS payload for 4-cartridge IIC printers with template variables for cloud_id, impression counts, uuid, timestamps
RTPDS-PayLoad-IPH.json — RTPDS payload for 2-cartridge IPH printers (CMY + K) with same template variables
address_data.json — 32 countries with 3 addresses each (2 valid, 1 invalid) for international shipping tests

Address data includes: Australia, Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Canada, Croatia, Cyprus, Czechia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hong Kong, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Puerto Rico, Romania, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, United Kingdom, United States

file_analyses.comment: Batch 16 contains 1 comprehensive page object for plan management with 60+ locators and dynamic helper methods, 2 RTPDS payload files for different printer types (IIC with 4 cartridges, IPH with 2 cartridges), 1 empty package init file, and 1 large address data file with 96 addresses across 32 countries for international testing. The UpdatePlanPage is highly reusable with systematic locator organization and three dynamic getter methods that enable data-driven plan selection tests. The RTPDS payloads use template variables for dynamic test data injection and represent complete printer status messages for supply fulfillment testing. The address data provides comprehensive coverage of all Instant Ink markets with both valid and invalid addresses for positive and negative testing scenarios.

file_analyses.commit_message: [Aava] Batch 16 analysis report | 5 files analyzed | 1 classes | 5 methods | 60 locators
